title: anemone alpina subsp. millefoliata
intro: Anemone alpina subsp. millefoliata is a subspecies of the anemone alpina plant, commonly known as a type of thimbleweed, belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, cuts, and skin irritations. The health benefits of anemone alpina subsp. millefoliata include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent infections, and promote tissue repair. An herbal preparation of the plant can be made by infusing its roots and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using it in a salve or cream to apply topically.
